primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si un engagement volontaire de neutralité carbone crée une obligation actuelle au sens d’IAS 37 pour les crédits carbone à acquérir ultérieurement, conduisant soit à une provision, soit à un passif éventuel.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: Only IAS 37 is provided as a candidate governing document, so no overlapping-document elimination was required; the issue is therefore resolved within the IAS 37 lineage, applying the stated precedence rule only insofar as no higher-precedence overlapping document is present.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low; the context directly includes IAS 37 definitions, recognition criteria, and the climate-related commitments agenda decision addressing the same type of voluntary emissions commitment.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define present obligation, constructive obligation, provisions, contingent liabilities, and the recognition threshold relevant to the question.

supporting_authority.reason: This agenda decision directly clarifies how IAS 37 applies to voluntary climate-related commitments and carbon-credit offsetting obligations.
These paragraphs help distinguish when an event or later change creates an obligating event rather than a mere intention or proposed future requirement.
This paragraph supports the probability assessment when similar obligations are considered as a class, though it is secondary to the core issue.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs distinguish provisions from trade payables and explain terminology, but they do not drive the peer treatment approaches for this issue.
This agenda decision concerns tax deposits and contingent assets, which is topically related to IAS 37 definitions but not relevant to carbon-credit provision approaches.
This agenda decision concerns government-imposed negative emission credits and helps by analogy, but the voluntary commitment fact pattern is addressed more directly elsewhere.

treatment_families.family: Recognised liability model under IAS 37 when a present obligation exists and recognition criteria are met
Unrecognised obligation model under IAS 37 when obligation existence or recognition criteria remain uncertain

approaches.label: Provision
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 37 treats a provision as the recognised model for a present legal or constructive obligation with probable outflow and reliable estimate, including offset obligations once the relevant emissions have occurred.

approaches.label: Contingent liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 37 treats a contingent liability as the peer unrecognised model when the obligation is only possible or when a present obligation fails the recognition criteria.
